effect: Low potassium
suggestions: Monitor potassium levels regularly.

effect: Beta blockers mask symptoms of low blood sugar.
suggestions: Check blood sugar more frequently.

effect: Calcium in milk reduces antibiotic absorption.
suggestions: Take doxycycline 1 hour before or 2 hours after milk.

effect: Antacids reduce ciprofloxacin absorption.
suggestions: Keep at least 2-hour gap.

effect: Increased stomach bleeding risk.
suggestions: Use stomach-protective medication if needed.

effect: Food reduces absorption, lowering thyroid levels.
suggestions: Take on empty stomach in the morning.

effect: High potassium levels.
suggestions: Regular blood tests to monitor potassium.

effect: Steroids increase blood sugar levels.
suggestions: Diabetes medicines may need dose adjustment.

effect: Increased sleepiness and slower reaction.
suggestions: Avoid alcohol while taking antihistamines.

effect: Increased risk of severe skin reactions.
suggestions: Doctor may start lamotrigine at a lower dose.

effect: Increased bleeding risk.
suggestions: Avoid combination unless prescribed with close monitoring.

effect: Higher chance of stomach irritation or ulcers.
suggestions: Take with food; doctor may recommend a stomach-protective medicine.

effect: Liver stress increases; paracetamol works less effectively.
suggestions: Limit paracetamol dose; consult doctor if taking long-term.

effect: Can increase risk of low blood sugar or lactic acidosis.
suggestions: Avoid heavy alcohol use while on metformin.

effect: Antibiotics may reduce pill effectiveness.
suggestions: Use backup contraception during antibiotic course.

effect: Excessive drowsiness, slowed breathing.
suggestions: Avoid taking together unless guided by a doctor.

effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome (agitation, sweating, fast heart rate).
suggestions: Use alternative painkiller if possible.

effect: Calcium reduces iron absorption.
suggestions: Take them 2 hours apart.

effect: Increased muscle pain risk due to high statin levels.
suggestions: Doctor may reduce simvastatin dose.

effect: Reduces clopidogrel effectiveness (blood thinner).
suggestions: Use a different acid reducer like pantoprazole.

effect: Increased statin levels causing muscle pain or kidney damage.
suggestions: Avoid combination; doctor may temporarily stop atorvastatin.

effect: May cause high potassium levels in the blood.
suggestions: Avoid extra potassium unless advised; monitor blood tests.

effect: Increases phenytoin levels causing toxicity.
suggestions: Monitor phenytoin levels; dose adjustment may be needed.

effect: Excessive sedation, slow breathing.
suggestions: Dose adjustment and monitoring may be required.

effect: Increased risk of liver damage.
suggestions: Regular liver tests; avoid long-term combined use.

effect: High risk of serotonin syndrome.
suggestions: Stop sertraline before starting linezolid under supervision.

effect: Propranolol blocks the effect of albuterol, reducing asthma relief.
suggestions: Switch to a more suitable beta-blocker if needed (e.g., atenolol).

effect: Decreases kidney function and increases potassium levels.
suggestions: Avoid frequent NSAID use; choose paracetamol instead.

effect: Carbamazepine reduces effectiveness of emergency contraception.
suggestions: Alternative contraception method may be required.

effect: Increased risk of movement disorders (tremors, stiffness).
suggestions: Avoid combination; choose alternate anti-nausea medicine.
